title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE TUKURAN TECHNICALГўВЂВ“VOCATIONAL HIGH SCHOOLГўВЂВ“TABUAN ANNEX IN BARANGAY TABUAN, MUNICIPALITY OF TUKURAN, PROVINCE OF ZAMBOANGA DEL SUR FROM THE TUKURAN TECHNICALГўВЂВ“VOCATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, TO BE KNOWN AS TABUAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 4918 ; Manila Bulletin (January 7, 2016); 112 OG No. 7, 742 (February 15, 2016)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10728, December 29, 2015 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE TUKURAN TECHNICALГўВЂВ“VOCATIONAL HIGH SCHOOLГўВЂВ“TABUAN ANNEX IN BARANGAY TABUAN, MUNICIPALITY OF TUKURAN, PROVINCE OF ZAMBOANGA DEL SUR FROM THE TUKURAN TECHNICALГўВЂВ“VOCATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, TO BE KNOWN AS TABUAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. The Tukuran Technical-Vocational High School ГўВЂВ“ Tabuan Annex in Barangay Tabuan, Municipality of Tukuran, Province of Zamboanga del Sur is hereby separated from the Tukuran Technical-Vocational High School and converted into an independent national high school to be known as Tabuan National High School.

SEC. 2. All personnel, assets, liabilities and records of the Tukuran Technical-Vocational High School ГўВЂВ“ Tabuan Annex are hereby transferred to and absorbed by the Tabuan National High School.

SEC. 3. The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the DepartmentГўВЂВ™s program the operationalization of the Tabuan National High School, the initial funding of which shall be charged against the current yearГўВЂВ™s appropriation of the Tukuran Technical-Vocational High School ГўВЂВ“ Tabuan Annex. Thereafter, the amount necessary for the continued operation of the school shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 4. The Secretary of Education shall issue rules and regulations necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 4. The Secretary of Education shall issue rules and regulations necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 5. This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette

or in a newspaper of general circulation.

Approved,

(Sgd.)

FRANKLIN M. DRILON

President of the Senate

(Sgd.)

FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on October 20, 2014 and October 6, 2015, respectively.

(Sgd.)

OSCAR G. YABES

Secretary of the Senate

(Sgd.)

MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved: DEC 29 2015

(Sgd.)

BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines
